subject: Ecology
title: Forests and Carbon Markets
description: A 60-second lesson on Forests and Carbon Markets.

hook.text: Forests absorb billions of tonnes of CO₂—so now companies pay to keep them standing.

buildup.text: Carbon credits let polluters offset emissions by funding forest conservation elsewhere.

discovery.text: REDD+ programs pay tropical nations to reduce deforestation and keep carbon locked in trees.

twist.text: Some carbon projects overestimate savings or protect forests that were never truly threatened.

climax.text: Credible forest carbon schemes require strict monitoring and rights for local communities.

punchline.text: Paying forests to stand is simple. Doing it honestly is hard.

quiz.question: What is a challenge with forest carbon credit programs?
options: Some overestimate savings or protect non-threatened forests
